'''Brady Malam''' (born 14 March 1973) is a New Zealand former [[rugby league]] footballer. His preferred position was at {{rlp|pr}}.

==Early years==
A junior from the [[Glenora Bears|Glenora]] club, Malam played for [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland]] at {{rlp|hk}} before bulking up and moving to {{rlp|pr}} in his late teens.<ref>[http://www.rugbyleague.co.nz/player.asp?id=113&teamID=4 Brady Malam] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081015070323/http://www.rugbyleague.co.nz/player.asp?id=113&teamID=4|date=15 October 2008}} ''rugbyleague.co.nz''</ref> He played for the [[Junior Kiwis]] in 1992 and 1993.<ref>Richard Becht. ''A New Breed Rising: The Warriors Winfield Cup Challenge''. Auckland, HarperCollins, 1994. {{ISBN|1-86950-154-3}}. p.182</ref> In the [[1994 Lion Red Cup]] Malam played one match for the [[Waitakere City Raiders]].<ref name=Raiders>''Lion Red Rugby League Annual 1994'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1994. p.121</ref>

==Auckland Warriors==
In 1996 he made his [[Australian Rugby League]] début for the [[Auckland Warriors]] and was a key part of the reserve grade team that made the grand final. He was also named Development Player of the Year. He went on to play 55 games for the Warriors, both from the bench and starting at prop. In 1997, during the [[Super League war]], he made his début for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in the [[Super League Tri-series|triseries]] against [[Queensland state rugby league team|Queensland]] and [[New South Wales state rugby league team|New South Wales]].

==Wigan Warriors==
In 2000 he moved to England to play for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>[https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/sport/rugby_league/575399.stm Wigan sign Auckland forward] ''BBC'', 22 December 1999</ref> He made 25 appearances and played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] from the interchange bench in their [[2000 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.]] He was released by the club at the end of the year.<ref>[https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/2999879/Super-League-Team-by-team-guide.html Super League Team-by-team guide] ''The Telegraph'', 20 June 2001</ref>
